# Pattern: Do-Not-Touch Detection

> **Purpose**: How to identify paths the executor MUST NOT modify.
> **MCP Validated**: 2026-05-19

## The principle

Every Task-Spec's Zone 3 must list specific paths the executor is FORBIDDEN from modifying. This is enforced at PR review and ideally at runtime.

## Categories to scan for

### Category 1 — Auto-generated files

Anything in:

```text
functions/**          ← auto-generated by build-copy-src.sh
dist/**               ← compiled output
build/**              ← compiled output
*.generated.{ts,py,go} ← code generators
__pycache__/**        ← Python cache
node_modules/**       ← package manager
```

These MUST be in Do-Not-Touch because edits will be obliterated by the next build.

### Category 2 — Configuration locked by upstream

```text
.github/workflows/**  ← CI/CD, usually owned by platform team
Dockerfile            ← infra team owns these
.dockerignore
.gitignore (sometimes)
infra/**, terraform/**, k8s/**
```

Edits here cross team boundaries. Out of scope for atomic tasks.

### Category 3 — Sibling modules out of scope

If the task is "Update ADF parser":

```text
src/core/parsers/d256_parser.py    ← sibling, do-not-touch
src/core/parsers/tddf_parser.py    ← sibling, do-not-touch
src/core/parsers/ach_parser.py     ← sibling, do-not-touch
```

Only `adf_parser.py` is in `touches_paths`. Everything else is do-not-touch.

### Category 4 — Auth, secrets, and credentials

```text
.env                  ← secrets, never committed
.env.example          ← may be edited but with caution
secrets/**            ← if anyone uses this convention
keys/**, certs/**
```

Touching credential files is almost always a mistake.

### Category 5 — Test fixtures used by other tests

```text
tests/fixtures/shared/**   ← shared across many tests
tests/golden/**            ← snapshot tests
```

Modifying these affects many tests beyond the current scope.

## The detection workflow (Phase 3 — host repo scan)

### Step 1 — Glob for auto-generated patterns

```bash
find . -path '*/functions/*' -o -path '*/dist/*' -o -path '*/build/*' \
       -o -path '*/__pycache__/*' -o -path '*/node_modules/*' \
       | head -20
```

Add anything found to Do-Not-Touch.

### Step 2 — Read CLAUDE.md / contributing docs

Project-specific do-not-touch rules often live there:

```bash
grep -i "do not\|don't\|never edit\|auto-generated" CLAUDE.md README.md CONTRIBUTING.md 2>/dev/null
```

### Step 3 — Identify sibling modules

If `touches_paths` is `src/core/parsers/adf_parser.py`, list its siblings:

```bash
ls src/core/parsers/*.py | grep -v adf_parser.py
```

Add them to Do-Not-Touch.

### Step 4 — Cross-cutting concerns

Look for in-flight tasks touching the same area:

```bash
grep -l 'src/core/parsers' tasks/T-*.md 2>/dev/null
```

If another task is in-flight on the same files, this task should either depend on it (`depends_on`) or be `parked` until the other completes.

## Example — concrete Do-Not-Touch list

For task "Update ADF parser for 2.56 spec":

```markdown
## Do-Not-Touch

Files the executor MUST NOT modify:

- `functions/adf/**` — auto-generated by `scripts/build-copy-src.sh`; edits will be overwritten
- `src/core/parsers/{d256,tddf,ach,ppm}_parser.py` — sibling parsers; out of scope
- `pipelines/**` — Lakeflow/Silver-side schema adaptation is a separate task
- `.github/workflows/**` — CI/CD owned by platform team
- `cd/**` — deploy matrix already configured for ADF; no changes needed
- Tests for non-ADF parsers (`tests/integration/{d256,tddf,ach,ppm}/`)
```

Six specific, actionable do-not-touch entries. Every one cites a reason.

## Anti-patterns in Do-Not-Touch lists

### Wrong: vague

```markdown
## Do-Not-Touch
- The database
- Important files
```

Vague. Doesn't bind the executor.

### Wrong: includes `touches_paths`

```markdown
## Do-Not-Touch
- src/core/parsers/adf_parser.py    ← this is in touches_paths!
```

Contradictory. The agent can't both edit and not-edit a file.

### Wrong: too restrictive

```markdown
## Do-Not-Touch
- src/**
- tests/**
- docs/**
```

If the task can't touch any source, tests, or docs — it can't do anything. Decompose or re-scope.

## Empty Do-Not-Touch is allowed

If no relevant paths exist (rare), write:

```markdown
## Do-Not-Touch

(none — this task is fully scoped to `touches_paths` with no adjacent risk)
```

Explicit empty is fine. Silence is not (rejected by validate-task-spec.sh).
